title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · Failure Case: Double Free Ownership
summary: An asynchronous job queue keeps aliases after cancellation and occasionally corrupts a newly allocated job at the same address. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem double free ownership increment in generation-checked handle repair. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
